entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Students learn the parts of speech through a four-part analysis.

This topic is included in the following course(s): Language Study: Grade 4

rows.label: Grammar: Grade 4
rows.books: Grammar Island||Grammar Island Teacher Manual (select pages)||Practice Island||Practice Island Teacher Manual (select pages)

rows.label: Language Study: Grade 4
days: Dictation: Grade 4*Penmanship & Copywork: Grade 4*Written Narration: Grade 4

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Grammar Island Pg. Numbers/Beginning Ending Quotes Ebook

blocks.text: Link back to the previous lesson.

Read the assigned pages and discuss new ideas. It is important for students to be able to talk about the concepts in their own words. The pictures and diagrams in the book can serve as a springboard for discussions about the grammar concepts.
Complete practice examples in the text and/or the practice book that accompany the lesson.
Observe examples of concepts in books being read and in daily life.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Introduction  
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ INTRO
Did you know that sentences do not occur in nature? Language comes from us; it is ourselves, in words!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.5-8 

➜ DISCUSS
How is a word different from a random group of letters, like "dixerp?"
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER TIP
 The key to explaining the four levels of grammar revolves around the noun and the verb. 

Parts of Speech: Noun and Verb
Parts of Sentence: Subject and Predicate
Phrases: No Subject Predicate allowed
Clauses: Subject Predicate required

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 2
lessons.body:
What Is Grammar? 
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.9-11

➜ DISCUSS
Think about a team sport. How are the players different from the game? How could this idea be applied to language?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 3
lessons.body:
The Parts of Speech  
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ INTRO
What do we mean when we say the "parts of a bird"? [the small bits that make up the whole bird] Our language has parts that make up the whole, too.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.13-19 

➜ DIAGRAM
Copy the diagram on p.17. Color the parts with crayons or colored pencils to match the drawing. Which parts seem most important?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 4
lessons.body:
Nouns 
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ RECAP
What are the two main kinds of words? [nouns and verbs]

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.20-21 

➜ PLAY
What would it be like if you could only talk in nouns? Try it! Can it work? [Remember, you can only use words that NAME things!]

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 5
lessons.body:
Pronouns
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ INTRO
How do we know when to use the word "I" and when to use "me"? Think of a sentence with each.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.22-24 

➜ DISCUSS
The prefix "pro" means "for." A "Pro-noun" is a word you use "for a noun." "Ante" means "before," and "cede" means "to go." So, the noun that "went before" the pronoun is called its "antecedent."
Read the left column on p.24 aloud and time it; read the right column aloud and time it. Pronouns are quick!

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Subject Pronouns
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.25-27 

➜ WRITE
Copy the lists of subject pronouns and object pronouns. Look at the last narration you wrote. Did you use any pronouns? Circle them with an aqua pencil.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
Nouns & Pronouns
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ INTRO
Two of the subject pronouns are "he" and "she." How do we know when to use which?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.28-29 

➜ DISCUSS
"She" is a feminine pronoun, so it needs to replace a feminine noun.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
Adjectives
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ INTRO
Quick! List as many kinds of tables as you can think of in one minute! Can you imagine if there was a whole separate noun for each one of these?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.30-33

➜ DISCUSS
Good writers use adjectives when they are needed. Too many adjectives make sentences too wordy and spoil the story.
Adjectives do not "describe" nouns from a distance but actually change them. The old car is a different car from the sports car. Adjectives modify.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
Adjectives (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Complete the activity on p.34 of Grammar Island. Why are some adjectives funny and others not funny?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.35-36 

➜ PRACTICE
Draw a purple box around each of the seven adjectives that modify the word "duck."

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions. Use a purple box for the adjective and an aqua circle for the pronouns. 
Grammar Island p.37-38  

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.39-41

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 11
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.42

➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions. Note that, for now, not every word will get a label. Check your work using the answer key and review any difficult concepts.
Grammar Island p.43

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 12
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions. Note that not all words will get a label. Check your work using the answer key and review any difficult concepts.
Grammar Island p.44

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.45

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 13
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Copy and color the noun system graphic on p.45. Explain why it is structured the way it is.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.46-47

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 14
lessons.body:
The Sentence
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.48-49  

➜ DIAGRAM
Copy the diagram on p.48 using colored pencils. Notice how simple it is!

➜ PRACTICE
Write your own short sentence like the one on p.49. Separate the two sides with a line.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 15
lessons.body:
Verbs
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.50-52 

➜ DISCUSS
On p.52, there are two sentences about ducks. One shows what the duck DOES. The other shows what the duck EQUALS. Can you figure out which is which?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 16
lessons.body:
Action Verbs
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.53-55 

➜ PRACTICE
For each subject, provide an action verb that makes sense:
1\. The kangaroo
2\. Mom
3\. A lion
4\. Our car

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
Linking Verbs
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.56-60 

➜ DISCUSS
In the sentence, "Sam has a hamster," why is "has" an action verb and not a linking verb?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 18
lessons.body:
Linking Verbs (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.61-62 

➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions to identify the action and linking verbs. Try replacing the verb with "=" to test.
Grammar Island p.63. Check your answers on p.64.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
Adverbs
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ RECAP
Some words, like "smells," can be either an action verb or a linking verb. How can that be? [ex. He smells a steak. / He smells good.] 

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.65-69  

➜ PRACTICE
Label the noun, verb, adjective, and adverb in the sentence on p.69.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
Adverbs (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.70-71  

➜ PRACTICE
Label each word in the sentences as pron., v., adj., n., or adv. Check your work using the Grammar Island Teacher Manual (See Quick Links).
Grammar Island p.72-73

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 21
lessons.body:
Conjunctions
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ INTRO
What is a "junction"? [a place where things come together, e.g., roads, railroads, etc.] Today's lesson is about a type of word that brings other words together.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.75-80 

➜ DISCUSS
What would our language sound like if we did not have conjunctions? Try it with the sentences on p.79.
On p.80, why does the third pair make a good conjunction when the first two do not?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
For more practice with Conjunctions, see the PDF.
∞ PDF Link: Grammar Island Additional Practice

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 22
lessons.body:
Making Sentences
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ PRACTICE
Create 5 sentences using the words on p.74. (Story-writing optional)
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Change lines when changing columns. Not every combination will work. Try variations until one does.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 23
lessons.body:
Exam Week
 
➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to the course.

Questions will come from:
Grammar Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 24
lessons.body:
Exam Week
 
➜ EXAMS 
Answer question(s) related to the course.

Questions will come from:
Grammar Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 25
lessons.body:
Prepositions
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.81-84  

➜ PRACTICE
Place a pencil somewhere in the room. Say three sentences to describe where the pencil is in space. What are the prepositions you used? Could you do it without using prepositions?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 26
lessons.body:
Prepositional Phrases
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ INTRO
Read the sentences on p.82 again. Notice the rhythm of the words in each prepositional phrase. Try clapping the rhythm.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.85-87 

➜ RECITE
Do you remember the object pronouns? Recite as many as you can. If you need help, look back on p.26.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 27
lessons.body:
Interjections
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.88-89

➜ DISCUSS
Interjections are unique; they do not interact with other words. They do not modify, replace, or join. Why would "yes" and "no" be interjections?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
If interested, let students write their own story or do it together. Pick a part of speech, and then write a story that illustrates the nature of that part.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 28
lessons.body:
Putting the Parts Together
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
•Go over each sentence on p.90-91 slowly and carefully, noticing how each word is labeled.
•Read p.92-93 

➜ PRACTICE
Since Bertie could only hear modifiers, she only heard parts of what was said to her. Go back and figure out what she heard.
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
For more practice with the eight parts of speech, see the PDF.
∞ PDF Link: Grammar Island Additional Practice

For groups or classrooms,
∞ PDF Link: 2 group games for Grammar Island

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 29
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions on p.94. ("Stories" need only be a few sentences.) Why will some combinations not work?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 30
lessons.body:
Sentences
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.95-100

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 31
lessons.body:
Direct Objects
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.101-106

➜ PRACTICE
Copy the sample sentence on p.103 using colored pencils. Copy the labels underneath. (Notice that they are on separate lines.)

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 32
lessons.body:
Indirect Objects
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.107-112 

➜ DISCUSS
Talk through the diagram on p.110.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 33
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.113 

➜ PRACTICE
Label the parts of speech and parts of the sentence on p.114. Check your work.

➜ DISCUSS
We can now do two-level analysis. What is the difference between level one and level two?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 34
lessons.body:
Linking Verbs
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.115-119 

➜ DISCUSS
• Remember to use the "=" test to identify linking verbs and subject complements.
• Why do we understand the subject more completely when there is a subject complement?

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 35
lessons.body:
Action & Linking Verbs
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.120-124  
Note that a subject complement can be a noun, a SUBJECT pronoun, or even an adjective. I am Joan; I am she; I am blue.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 36
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions on p.125 of Grammar Island. ("Stories" need only be a few sentences.)

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.126
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
For more practice with direct objects and subject complements and Level Two sentence analysis, see the PDF. 
∞ PDF Link: Grammar Island Additional Practice

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 37
lessons.body:
Prepositional Phrases
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.128-132 

➜ PRACTICE
Write one sentence in which a prepositional phrase modifies a noun. Write another in which it modifies a verb.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 38
lessons.body:
Prepositional Phrases (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Follow the instructions on p.133 of Grammar Island. ("Stories" need only be a few sentences.)

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 39
lessons.body:
Prepositional Phrases (cont.)
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.134-135
lessons.teacherNotes:
For more practice with prepositional phrases and Level Three sentence analysis, see the PDF. 
∞ PDF Link: Grammar Island Additional Practice

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 40
lessons.body:
Clauses
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.137-141

➜ PRACTICE
Write a sentence that has two clauses.

➜ NOTE
A key concept is that the phrases are INSIDE the clauses. The clause includes the phrase. The phrase is a part of its clause.

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 41
lessons.body:
Compound Sentences
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Grammar Island p.142-146

➜ PRACTICE
Write a compound sentence. Remember the comma!

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 42
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Grammar Island
 
➜ INTRO
Read p.147 in Grammar Island and study the sentence.

➜ PRACTICE
Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment. Check your work. Grammar Island p.148-149

lessons.title: 15m Grammar: Grade 4 - Lesson 49
lessons.body:
Practice
⍞ Materials: Practice Island
 
➜ PRACTICE
Label parts of speech, parts of the sentence, phrases, and clauses. Write a comment. Check your work. Practice Island Sentences 1-2  
∞ PDF Link: Practice Island Answer Key
lessons.teacherNotes: Go at your student's pace as you work together through these sentences. It is better to take the time to talk through each sentence and ensure the student understands each part than to get through lots of sentences with a lower level of understanding.
